is a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") (called *[labor union](/wiki/Labor_union "Labor union")* in American English) which represents workers from all industries and companies, rather than just one organisation or a particular sector, as in a [craft union](/wiki/Craft_union "Craft union") or [industrial union](/wiki/Industrial_union "Industrial union"). A general union differs from a [union federation](/wiki/Union_federation "Union federation") or [trades council](/wiki/Trades_council "Trades council") in that its members are individuals, not unions. The creation of general unions, from the early nineteenth century in the United Kingdom and somewhat later elsewhere, occurred around the same time as efforts began to unionise workers in new industries, in particular those where employment could be irregular.

Dyrenfurth, Nick (2017). [*A Powerful Influence on Australian Affairs: A New History of the AWU*](https://books.google.com/books?id=HAl0DgAAQBAJ). [Carlton](/wiki/Carlton,_Victoria "Carlton, Victoria"): [Melbourne University Publishing](/wiki/Melbourne_University_Publishing "Melbourne University Publishing"). [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [9780522872071](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9780522872071 "Special:BookSources/9780522872071").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

All-sector bargaining entity

This article is about the type of trade union. For other uses, see [General Union](/wiki/General_Union_(disambiguation) "General Union (disambiguation)").

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Globe icon. | The examples and perspective in this article **may not represent a [worldwide view](/wiki/Wikipedia:WikiProject_Countering_systemic_bias "Wikipedia:WikiProject Countering systemic bias") of the subject**. You may [improve this article](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=General_union&action=edit), discuss the issue on the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:General_union "Talk:General union"), or [create a new article](/wiki/Wikipedia:Article_wizard "Wikipedia:Article wizard"), as appropriate. *(November 2022)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/General_union "Special:EditPage/General union") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["General union"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22General+union%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22General+union%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22General+union%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22General+union%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22General+union%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22General+union%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2021)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

A **general union** is a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") (called *[labor union](/wiki/Labor_union "Labor union")* in American English) which represents workers from all industries and companies, rather than just one organisation or a particular sector, as in a [craft union](/wiki/Craft_union "Craft union") or [industrial union](/wiki/Industrial_union "Industrial union"). A general union differs from a [union federation](/wiki/Union_federation "Union federation") or [trades council](/wiki/Trades_council "Trades council") in that its members are individuals, not unions. The creation of general unions, from the early nineteenth century in the United Kingdom and somewhat later elsewhere, occurred around the same time as efforts began to unionise workers in new industries, in particular those where employment could be irregular.

Proponents of general unions claim that their broader range of members allows more opportunities for [solidarity action](/wiki/Solidarity_action "Solidarity action") and better coordination in [general strikes](/wiki/General_strike "General strike") and the like. Detractors claim that the broader remit means they tend to be more [bureaucratic](/wiki/Bureaucracy "Bureaucracy") and respond less effectively to events in a single industry.

In the United Kingdom, general unions include the [GMB](/wiki/GMB_(trade_union) "GMB (trade union)") and the [Transport and General Workers' Union](/wiki/Transport_and_General_Workers%27_Union "Transport and General Workers' Union"). In Australia a good example of a general union is the [Australian Workers' Union](/wiki/Australian_Workers%27_Union "Australian Workers' Union").[[1]](#cite_note-Dyrenfurth-1): 2
